title: Morning Ignition
intro: Not every morning needs a full journal. Some mornings need a single, fast, visceral reset that takes 90 seconds and gets your state to where the work can begin. This is that. Not a substitute for the daily journal — a complement for the days when full journaling isn't happening.

quote.text: The first 90 seconds of your working day determine the next four hours. Spend them deliberately.

deliverable: A one-line statement of what you are refusing today, plus a three-word activation phrase you read aloud.

exercises.title: The 90-Second Ignition
exercises.sub: 90 sec · do this at your desk before opening email
prompt: 1. The ONE thing I am doing today (not five — one): ...
2. The thing I am refusing to be pulled into today: ...
3. The three-word phrase I say to myself right now, out loud, before I open anything: ...

guidance.title: Say the phrase aloud
guidance.body: The phrase is the ignition. It has to be said out loud, in your own voice, in the room you are in. Written phrases don't fire the state change — spoken ones do. It can feel silly the first few days. That passes.

guidance.title: The refusal is doing real work
guidance.body: Most lost mornings are lost to the thing you let pull you in — a Slack, a news feed, a meeting you should have declined. Naming the refusal by name in advance is 80% of resisting it in the moment. You are pre-paying the willpower.

guidance.title: The ONE thing matters
guidance.body: Not a list. One. If you finish it, the day is won regardless of what else happens. This single rule quietly reorders the entire day.

exercises.watch_out: Letting this creep toward a full daily journal. If it takes more than two minutes, you are doing the wrong exercise. Save the long-form for the daily/therapy modes — this one is disciplined brevity.
